item.text: {"case_id":"mtj-026","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":true,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"The answer correctly wakes up to the WAE/control-allocation issue: this is not just a prompt or wording problem but an unauthorized external refund commitment. It assigns approval as a hard workflow constraint, narrows agent judgment to eligibility/explanation/case creation, and moves the evidence gate before customer-facing commitment by requiring approved status, approval ID, and approved actor before refund language can be sent. It explicitly says prompt changes are insufficient and adds audits/regression tests. No fail signal of only tuning the prompt."}
